user_message: Just came out of security posture with Iron Creek Mining. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 11:13 EST
Subject: Re: Re: security posture Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 09:33 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 12:14 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-H trap (Skips the question 'why did they reach out now'); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-H failure mode (Skips the question 'why did they reach out now')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-H -- Skips the question 'why did they reach out now'
